gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: updateTokenPoolInfo lacks access-control role check in Portal.sol wrapper, delegating to unverified PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER implementation
candidate.claimed_root_cause: Portal.sol applies only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH) on updateTokenPoolInfo; DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE enforcement exists only in interface declaration and may be absent in the PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall target
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged attacker overwrites _dexPools[T] with attacker-controlled fake pool, causing subsequent swapExactInput calls to route through attacker pool and steal quote tokens from swappers

live_preconditions.name: portal_not_halted
live_preconditions.observed_value: 1361.66 BNB live balance implies portal is active; bitFlags value not directly observed

live_preconditions.name: token_with_status_DEX_exists
live_preconditions.observed_value: Not confirmed from live_context.json; LaunchedToDEX events not decoded in seed

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER_address
live_preconditions.observed_value: Not directly readable; defined in PortalBase.sol storage but value not in live_context.json

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER_enforces_DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE
live_preconditions.observed_value: UNKNOWN — PortalDexRouter.sol is not present in source_identity.source_files; DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE appears only in src/interfaces/IPortal.sol (2 matches) and in zero implementation files (Portal.sol, PortalBase.sol, PortalCommon.sol)
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: Decompile or obtain verified source for PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER address; or run Foundry fork test at block 108382650 calling updateTokenPoolInfo from unprivileged EOA and observe revert vs success

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Confirmed: only guard is onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH); no onlyRole or hasRole check present in the Portal.sol wrapper layer

Role is declared in the interface only; grep across all implementation source files (Portal.sol, PortalBase.sol, PortalCommon.sol) returns zero matches, meaning enforcement must be inside the unverified PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall target if it exists at all
Contrast: setBitFlags uses onlyRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE); updateTokenPoolInfo does not, making the absence of a role check anomalous relative to other config-mutating functions
PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER is an address stored in PortalBase; its implementation contract source is absent from source_identity.source_files, making the role-check question unresolvable from source alone

attacker_path.actor: Any unprivileged EOA with no roles
attacker_inputs: token = any address T where getTokenV6(T).status == TokenStatus.DEX
call_sequence: _delegateToImpl(PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER) executes PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER bytecode in Portal storage context
IF PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER lacks DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE check: _dexPools[T] is overwritten with fakePool
Subsequent swapExactInput(T, ...) routes through fakePool, which can return manipulated amounts or steal tokens
state_after: swapExactInput calls for T route through fakePool

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: Quote tokens (BNB or ERC20) paid by users calling swapExactInput for token T
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: FakePool contract receives and retains quote tokens that should go to the legitimate DEX pool and swappers
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Swappers lose quote tokens; Portal's DEX routing integrity is broken for token T
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker has no entitlement to pool configuration changes or to intercept swap proceeds; the DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE is the declared gating mechanism and its absence from Portal.sol means the only backstop is the unverified PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER implementation

setup: Fork BSC at block 108382650 against proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
Query LaunchedToDEX events to obtain a token T with status == TokenStatus.DEX
Deploy a minimal FakePool contract that implements the expected pool interface
Create unprivileged attacker EOA (vm.addr(1)) with no roles granted
transaction_sequence: portal.updateTokenPoolInfo(T, PackedDexPool({pool: address(fakePool), fee: 0, poolType: PoolType.V2, tickSpacing: 0, unused: 0}))
expected_assertions: If call reverts with access-control error: REJECT — PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER enforces the role, finding is invalid
If call succeeds: assert portal.getTokenV6(T).pool == address(fakePool) — confirms _dexPools[T] was overwritten, finding is HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If updateTokenPoolInfo reverts with any access-control or role-related error from the PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall target, the finding must be REJECTED as the role check exists in the implementation layer

rejection_reason: Cannot be promoted beyond NEEDS_LOCAL_PROOF because the critical gate — whether PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER enforces DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE — is unresolvable from available source. PortalDexRouter.sol is absent from source_identity.source_files. DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE has zero matches in all implementation files. The Portal.sol wrapper confirms no role check at that layer, but the delegatecall target is the actual enforcement point and its bytecode has not been verified or decompiled. A Foundry fork test against BSC mainnet is the minimum required proof step. concrete_fund_or_reward_gain and gain_is_beyond_entitlement cannot be set true until the fork test confirms the call succeeds without revert.
